# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create Kafka broker configuration file (server.properties) for production deployment. Configure listeners, log directories, and replication settings. Set up Zookeeper connection and broker ID. This is for our distributed Kafka cluster.
# Category: kafka_topic_authorization

# Apache Kafka Broker Configuration - Production Deployment
# server.properties

############################# Server Basics #############################

# The id of the broker. This must be set to a unique integer for each broker.
broker.id=1

# Switch to enable topic deletion or not
delete.topic.enable=true

# Enable auto creation of topics on the server
auto.create.topics.enable=false

############################# Socket Server Settings #############################

# The address the socket server listens on.
# hostname:port for clients to connect to
listeners=PLAINTEXT://0.0.0.0:9092,SSL://0.0.0.0:9093

# Hostname and port the broker will advertise to producers and consumers.
advertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://kafka-broker-1.example.com:9092,SSL://kafka-broker-1.example.com:9093

# Listener security protocol mapping
listener.security.protocol.map=PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT,SSL:SSL

# The inter-broker communication protocol
inter.broker.listener.name=PLAINTEXT

# The number of threads that the server uses for receiving requests from the network
num.network.threads=8

# The number of threads that the server uses for processing requests
num.io.threads=16

# The send buffer (SO_SNDBUF) used by the socket server
socket.send.buffer.bytes=102400

# The receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) used by the socket server
socket.receive.buffer.bytes=102400

# The maximum size of a request that the socket server will accept (protection against OOM)
socket.request.max.bytes=104857600

# Maximum number of connections allowed per IP address (0 = unlimited)
max.connections.per.ip=256

# The maximum number of queued requests allowed before blocking network threads
queued.max.requests=500

############################# Log / Data Directory Settings #############################

# A comma separated list of directories under which to store log files
# Use multiple directories on separate physical disks for better performance
log.dirs=/var/kafka/data-1,/var/kafka/data-2,/var/kafka/data-3

# The default number of log partitions per topic
num.partitions=6

# The number of threads per data directory to be used for log recovery at startup and flushing at shutdown
num.recovery.threads.per.data.dir=2

############################# Replication Settings #############################

# Default replication factor for automatically created topics
default.replication.factor=3

# Minimum number of replicas that must acknowledge a write for the write to be considered successful
min.insync.replicas=2

# The replication factor for the offsets topic
offsets.topic.replication.factor=3

# The replication factor for the transaction state log topic
transaction.state.log.replication.factor=3

# Override min.insync.replicas for the transaction state log topic
transaction.state.log.min.isr=2

# The replication factor for the group metadata internal topic "__consumer_offsets"
# For production, higher replication factor (e.g., 3) ensures availability
offsets.topic.num.partitions=50

# Leader imbalance check interval in seconds
leader.imbalance.check.interval.seconds=300

# The ratio of leader imbalance allowed per broker
leader.imbalance.per.broker.percentage=10

# Maximum number of replicas that can be concurrently fetched
num.replica.fetchers=4

# The number of bytes of messages to attempt to fetch for each partition
replica.fetch.max.bytes=1048576

# The maximum wait time for each fetcher request issued by follower replicas
replica.fetch.wait.max.ms=500

# If a follower hasn't sent any fetch requests or consumed up to the leaders log end offset
# for at least this time, the leader will remove the follower from ISR
replica.lag.time.max.ms=30000

# Unclean leader election - DISABLE in production for data consistency
unclean.leader.election.enable=false

############################# Log Flush Policy #############################

# The number of messages accumulated on a log partition before messages are flushed to disk
# Using OS-level flushing is recommended for production; set high values here
log.flush.interval.messages=10000

# The maximum time in ms that a message in any topic is kept in memory before being flushed to disk
log.flush.interval.ms=1000

# The frequency in milliseconds that the log flusher checks whether any log needs to be flushed to disk
log.flush.scheduler.interval.ms=3000

############################# Log Retention Policy #############################

# The minimum age of a log file to be eligible for deletion due to age
log.retention.hours=168

# The maximum size of a log segment file. When this size is reached a new log segment will be created
log.segment.bytes=1073741824

# The interval at which log segments are checked to see if they can be deleted
log.retention.check.interval.ms=300000

# A size-based retention policy for logs (optional - uncomment to enable)
# log.retention.bytes=1073741824

# The maximum size of the log before deleting it (per partition)
# -1 means no size limit
log.retention.bytes=-1

# Enable log compaction for topics that require it
log.cleanup.policy=delete

# The maximum time a message will remain ineligible for compaction in the log
log.cleaner.min.compaction.lag.ms=0

# Enable the log cleaner process to run on the server
log.cleaner.enable=true

# The total memory used for log deduplication across all cleaner threads
log.cleaner.dedupe.buffer.size=134217728

# The number of background threads to use for log cleaning
log.cleaner.threads=2

############################# Zookeeper Settings #############################

# Zookeeper connection string - comma separated host:port pairs
zookeeper.connect=zk-1.example.com:2181,zk-2.example.com:2181,zk-3.example.com:2181/kafka-production

# Timeout in ms for connecting to zookeeper
zookeeper.connection.timeout.ms=18000

# Zookeeper session timeout
zookeeper.session.timeout.ms=18000

# Maximum number of unacknowledged requests the client will send to Zookeeper before blocking
zookeeper.max.in.flight.requests=10

############################# Group Coordinator Settings #############################

# The delay in milliseconds for the initial consumer rebalance
group.initial.rebalance.delay.ms=3000

# The maximum allowed session timeout for registered consumers
group.max.session.timeout.ms=1800000

# The minimum allowed session timeout for registered consumers
group.min.session.timeout.ms=6000

############################# Transaction Settings #############################

# The maximum amount of time in ms that the transaction coordinator will wait
# before proactively aborting ongoing transactions
transaction.max.timeout.ms=900000

# Transactional ID expiration time
transactional.id.expiration.ms=604800000

############################# SSL Configuration (if SSL listener is enabled) #############################

# SSL keystore configuration
ssl.keystore.location=/etc/kafka/ssl/kafka.broker.keystore.jks
ssl.keystore.password=${SSL_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD}
ssl.key.password=${SSL_KEY_PASSWORD}

# SSL truststore configuration
ssl.truststore.location=/etc/kafka/ssl/kafka.broker.truststore.jks
ssl.truststore.password=${SSL_TRUSTSTORE_PASSWORD}

# SSL protocol settings
ssl.enabled.protocols=TLSv1.3,TLSv1.2
ssl.protocol=TLSv1.3
ssl.client.auth=required

# SSL endpoint identification algorithm (set to empty string to disable hostname verification)
ssl.endpoint.identification.algorithm=https

############################# Metrics and Monitoring #############################

# Enable JMX reporting
# Set JMX_PORT environment variable to enable JMX (e.g., JMX_PORT=9999)

# Metrics reporters (uncomment and configure for your monitoring stack)
# metric.reporters=com.linkedin.kafka.cruisecontrol.metricsreporter.CruiseControlMetricsReporter

# The metrics sample window size
metrics.num.samples=2
metrics.sample.window.ms=30000

# The metrics recording level
metrics.recording.level=INFO

############################# Request / Connection Settings #############################

# The maximum number of connections allowed from each IP
# max.connections.per.ip.overrides=

# Idle connections timeout: the server will close idle connections after this
connections.max.idle.ms=600000

# The maximum request size the broker will allow
message.max.bytes=10485760

# The maximum size of a message that can be fetched by a consumer
# Should be at least as large as message.max.bytes
replica.fetch.max.bytes=10485760

# Request timeout for controller operations
controller.socket.timeout.ms=30000

# The amount of time to wait before a request times out
request.timeout.ms=30000

############################# Background Thread Settings #############################

# The number of background threads to use for various background processing tasks
background.threads=10

############################# Controlled Shutdown Settings #############################

# Enable controlled shutdown of the broker
controlled.shutdown.enable=true

# Number of retries for controlled shutdown
controlled.shutdown.max.retries=3

# Backoff time between controlled shutdown retries
controlled.shutdown.retry.backoff.ms=5000

############################# Rack Awareness (for multi-AZ/datacenter deployments) #############################

# Configure rack ID for rack-aware replica assignment
# broker.rack=us-east-1a
